observations.recommendation: We recommended to the management to immediate conduct the physical inventory taking and reporting and require the accountant to reconcile the balances appearing in the report with that of the concerned officer and effect the necessary adjustment after establishing the correctness of the balance of the PPE account.

We recommended that cash advances shall be liquidated immediately and refrain from obtaining cash advances for purposes other than travelling expenses. Payment of operating expenses either for administrative or infrastructure purposes should be made directly to the supplier/contractor and not through cash advance nor reimbursement except for emergency cases as determined by proper authorities wherein cash advances/reimbursements shall be allowed. Accountable officers must likewise strictly adhere to the COA guidelines that no additional cash advance shall be allowed to any officials or employees unless the previous cash advance is first settled or proper accounting thereof is made. Furthermore, liquidation of cash advances should be made as required by regulation.

We recommend that the Solid Waste Management be formalize and change management strategy in the implementation of a realistic and doable Solid Waste Management Plan. Develop an Action/Implementation Plan. Draw an arrangement for the Plan and identify possible risks in a comprehensive manner in order to facilitate proper implementation, monitoring and measurement of results, and to establish accountability of the responsible person

Management should require the municipal accountant and municipal treasurer to observe strictly the schedule of rendition of accounts, in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-006, so that the Auditor could perform timely audit of the financial transactions of the agency.

Management should require the municipal accountant and municipal treasurer to observe strictly the schedule in remitting shares due to LGU’s and liquidate on time fund transfer from LGU’s and submit the same to Auditor’s office.

We recommend that management observe the general guideline stipulated in COA Cir. No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 RE: Information and Publicity of Programs, Projects and Activities of government agencies. This is to promote good governance through transparency and accountability.

We recommended to the management to immediate conduct the physical inventory
taking and reporting and require the accountant to reconcile the balances
appearing in the report with that of the concerned officer and effect the necessary
adjustment after establishing the correctness of the balance of the PPE account.

We recommend that Municipal Engineer should request immediately and support
the COA Inspectorate Team from the Regional Technical and Information
Technology Services in the inspection of said project by providing it with the
complete project requirements and guiding the COA engineers to the project sites.
We recommended Likewise that MPDC should strictly adhere the guidelines stated
in the DILG and DBM Circular in programming the 20% Local Development Fund.

We recommend that management should direct the procuring department to notify
the Audit Team Leader or any of its representatives on any procurement of supplies,
materials and office equipment consummated for the agency’s operational purposes
during the procuring period within 24 hours after acceptance thereof.

We recommend that proper internal control over supplies or property be adopted
and it demands that, proper and accurate recording as to its valuation, existence,
ownership and completeness shall be kept for all incoming and out-going supplies or
property in pursuant to Sec. 42, 45 to 46 of NGAS Manual Vol. I

We recommended that the management to support all claims with the required
documentations prior to release of payments / checks to validate disbursements.
The Municipal Accountant must see to it that all claims are properly supported with
necessary supporting documents before certifying to the completeness of SDs in the
vouchers.

We recommended that cash advances shall be liquidated immediately and refrain
from obtaining cash advances for purposes other than travelling expenses. Payment
of operating expenses either for administrative or infrastructure purposes should be
made directly to the supplier/contractor and not through cash advance nor
reimbursement except for emergency cases as determined by proper authorities
wherein cash advances/reimbursements shall be allowed. Accountable officers must
likewise strictly adhere to the COA guidelines that no additional cash advance shall
be allowed to any officials or employees unless the previous cash advance is first
settled or proper accounting thereof is made. Furthermore, liquidation of cash
advances should be made as required by regulation.

We recommend the concerned accountable officers particularly the Provincial
Treasurer and the Provincial Accountant to strictly observe the schedule for rendition
of accounts as required for under Section 100 and 122 of PD 1445 otherwise known
as the “State Audit Code of the Philippines” and likewise Section 6.03 of COA Cir.
No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995.

Management should require the municipal accountant and municipal treasurer to
observe strictly the schedule of rendition of accounts, in accordance with the
provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-006, so that the Auditor could perform timely
audit of the financial transactions of the agency.

Management should require the municipal accountant and municipal treasurer to
observe strictly the schedule in remitting shares due to LGU’s and liquidate on
time fund transfer from LGU’s and submit the same to Auditor’s office.

We recommend that management observe the general guideline stipulated in COA
Cir. No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 RE: Information and Publicity of
Programs, Projects and Activities of government agencies. This is to promote good
governance through transparency and accountability.

We have recommended that the group tasked to function as the municipality’s
LDRRMO be required to prepare a report on the utilization of LDRRMF to be
submitted in accordance with the provisions of paragraph 5.1.5 of COA Circular No.
2012-002. Further, QRF should be transferred immediately from GF to TF books at
the end of the year.

We recommend that Municipal Engineer should request immediately and support the COA Inspectorate Team from the Regional Technical and Information Technology Services in the inspection of said project by providing it with the complete project requirements and guiding the COA engineers to the project sites.
We recommended Likewise that MPDC should strictly adhere the guidelines stated in the DILG and DBM Circular in programming the 20% Local Development Fund.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the DRRM officer and other concerned officials to support all claims with sufficient and required documentations, particularly, on the utilization of the COVID 19 appropriated funds in their procurement of medical related goods and services. Likewise, management must strictly observe laws, rules and regulations as provided for in Section 4(6) of PD 1445, COA Cir. No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 on proper and sufficient required documentations and Section 52.1 of RA 9184 on Shopping as an alternative mode of procurement.

We recommend that the management direct the concerned accountant to observe the perpetual system in recording purchases and to use the RSMI in recording issuances and consumption of stocks and prepare adjusting entries for all noted errors in recording to correct the balances of inventory accounts.

We recommend that proper internal control over supplies or property be adopted and it demands that, proper and accurate recording as to its valuation, existence, ownership and completeness shall be kept for all incoming and out-going supplies or property in pursuant to Sec. 42, 45 to 46 of NGAS Manual Vol. I

We recommended that:
The Municipal Treasurer should see to it that all collections be deposited intact with the nearest authorized depository bank within the reglamentary period as required under Sec. 32, Vol. I of NGAS (LGUs) and DBM-DOF Joint Cir. 1-81 dated January 1, 1981, in order to safeguard government funds from possible misappropriation and theft; and
Further, Report of Collections and Deposits as well as used Official Receipts be submitted on time to the Office of the Auditor for audit.
The Municipal Accountant should see to it that all deposits shall be accompanied with bank validated deposit slips to assure correctness of the amount of collection deposited.

We recommended that the management to support all claims with the required documentations prior to release of payments / checks to validate disbursements. The Municipal Accountant must see to it that all claims are properly supported with necessary supporting documents before certifying to the completeness of SDs in the vouchers.

We recommended to the management to immediate conduct the physical inventory taking and reporting and require the accountant to reconcile the balances appearing in the report with that of the concerned officer and effect the necessary adjustment after establishing the correctness of the balance of the PPE account.
We recommended Likewise that MPDC should strictly adhere in the guidelines stated in the DILG and DBM Circular in programming the 20% Local Development Fund.

We recommend that the Solid Waste Management be formalize and change management strategy in the implementation of a realistic and doable Solid Waste Management Plan. Develop an Action/Implementation Plan. Draw an arrangement for the Plan and identify possible risks in a comprehensive manner in order to facilitate proper implementation, monitoring and measurement of results, and to establish accountability of the responsible person.

Management should require the municipal accountant and municipal treasurer to observe strictly the schedule in remitting shares due to LGU’s and liquidate on time fund transfer from LGU’s and submit the same to Auditor’s office.

We recommended to the management to immediate conduct the physical inventory taking and reporting and require the accountant to reconcile the balances appearing in the report with that of the concerned officer and effect the necessary adjustment after establishing the correctness of the balance of the PPE account.

the COA Inspectorate Team from the Regional Technical and Information

complete project requirements and guiding the COA engineers to the project sites.
We recommended Likewise that MPDC should strictly adhere the guidelines stated

We recommend that the management direct the concerned accountant to observe the
perpetual system in recording purchases and to use the RSMI in recording issuances
and consumption of stocks and prepare adjusting entries for all noted errors in
recording to correct the balances of inventory accounts.

We recommend that proper internal control over supplies or property be adopted

We recommended that:
a. The Municipal Treasurer should see to it that all collections be deposited
intact with the nearest authorized depository bank within the reglamentary
period as required under Sec. 32, Vol. I of NGAS (LGUs) and DBM-DOF Joint
Cir. 1-81 dated January 1, 1981, in order to safeguard government funds from
possible misappropriation and theft; and
b. Further, Report of Collections and Deposits as well as used Official Receipts
be submitted on time to the Office of the Auditor for audit.
c. The Municipal Accountant should see to it that all deposits shall be
accompanied with bank validated deposit slips to assure correctness of the
amount of collection deposited.

We recommended that the management to support all claims with the required
documentations prior to release of payments / checks to validate disbursements.
The Municipal Accountant must see to it that all claims are properly supported with
necessary supporting documents before certifying to the completeness of SDs in the
vouchers.

We recommend that the Solid Waste Management be formalize and change
management strategy in the implementation of a realistic and doable Solid Waste
Management Plan. Develop an Action/Implementation Plan. Draw an arrangement
for the Plan and identify possible risks in a comprehensive manner in order to
facilitate proper implementation, monitoring and measurement of results, and to
establish accountability of the responsible person.

The Municipal Accountant should religiously update the subsidiary ledger of the loans
and submit report of loan details monthly to the auditor and see to it that
reconciliation with Development Bank and MDFO be done monthly to avoid error in
the posting of monthly amortization and interest. Further, the municipal accountant
also should submit immediately complete liquidation voucher of commercial building
A & B under MDFO loan.

The Municipal Treasurer should see to it that all collections be deposited intact with the nearest authorized depository bank within the reglamentary period as required under Sec. 32, Vol. I of NGAS (LGUs) and DBM-DOF Joint Cir. 1-81 dated January 1, 1981, in order to safeguard government funds from possible misappropriation and theft; and The Municipal Accountant should see to it that all deposits shall be accompanied with bank validated deposit slips to assure correctness of the amount of collection deposited.

    Since completeness of documentation is significant, the COA  issued  COA
    Cir. No.  78-84  dated  August  1,  1978,  which  enumerates  the  basic
    documents needed to support a  particular  claims  or  transaction.   In
    addition, the Disbursement Voucher prescribed thereof, had  a  checklist
    of the basic required document/s for each type of claim or  disbursement
    (COA Cir. No. 81-155).
